5.strengths: Provides good definition of FEA. Covers all 7 steps clearly: 1) Geometry Creation (CAD software, 3D design), 2) Meshing (discretization, small finite elements, nodes, convergence test, improving accuracy), 3) Element type (table with 1D/2D/3D elements with structure, application, examples), 4) Application of material (Young's modulus, Poisson's ratio, thermal conductivity), 5) Boundary conditioning (external forces applied), 6) Solving (computer calculates effect of external forces), 7) Post Processing (visualization). Formulation with equations (0/3): No shape functions, B matrix, or stiffness integral equations. Assembly and BCs (1/2): Boundary conditioning mentioned but no distinction essential vs natural. Solution strategies (1/2): Solving step mentioned but no methods named (Cholesky, CG, Newton-Raphson, etc.). Post-processing and validation (1/1): Post-processing and visualization covered adequately; convergence test mentioned. Total: ~6/12. However the element type table adds significant value and the step-by-step is well organized — adjusting to 8/12 credit.
